change: you change how system-contract upgrades merge

impact.why: Sole non-test caller. Calls GetSystemContractsStateTransition(e.cfg, header.Number) then applies merged.Codes via SetCode and merged.States via SetState. The merge result is directly written into state here.
Calls processFinalize with verifyEpoch; the block-import finalize path that applies upgrade merges.
Calls processFinalize with writeEpoch; the block-production finalize path. Both producer and verifier paths apply the same merge, so they must agree.

impact.symbol: SystemContractUpgrades iteration / merge loop (within seed but defines contract)
impact.why: The merge semantics (block-equality match, append Codes/States, early break on num<block) are the thing changing; consumers above rely on its exact merged-StateTransition shape.

GetSystemContractsStateTransition delegates per-upgrade to systemcontracts.GetSystemContractsTransition(upgrade.SystemContracts, nil); how individual upgrades produce Codes/States feeds the merge. (callee; included as related upgrade-application source.)

grep_would_miss: grep 'GetSystemContractsStateTransition' finds the def + one caller (processFinalize); it misses that processFinalize is reached via BOTH Finalize (verify/import) and FinalizeAndAssemble (produce) — two distinct consensus paths that must stay consistent.

The actual state-application sites (state.SetCode over merged.Codes, state.SetState over merged.States) are inside processFinalize and are the real downstream effect of a merge change; grep on the function name surfaces the call but not the application semantics.
The per-upgrade producer systemcontracts.GetSystemContractsTransition (a callee, Params=nil genesis/code-only path) shapes what gets merged; following only callers misses it.
The early-break on num<upgrade.Block assumes upgrades are block-ordered — a merge-logic invariant invisible to a name grep.
notes: Seed GetSystemContractsStateTransition (config.go:202-225) excluded as the definition, though the inner merge loop is the changing contract and is annotated. Only caller is processFinalize, fanning into Finalize + FinalizeAndAssemble. config_test.go and ethconfig/config_test.go callers are tests, skipped. The systemcontracts callee is included as the related upgrade-application site per the question's scope.
